hero.title: Run your Amazon operations from one place.
hero.subtitle: The operating system for product businesses managing suppliers, purchase orders, inventory and profit.

screenshots.hero_alt: Freedoliapp dashboard with KPIs, active alerts and revenue trends.

suppliers.title: Organise suppliers in a single workspace.
suppliers.description: Keep supplier details, terms and communication in one place so your team always knows who you buy from and on what conditions.
suppliers.alt: Freedoliapp suppliers grid showing supplier cards and key details.

orders_workflow.title: Track purchase orders from quote to delivery.
orders_workflow.description: Create and follow purchase orders through production and shipment so stock arrives when you need it.
orders_workflow.alt: Freedoliapp purchase order workflow cards with progress states.

decisions.title: See the decisions that matter next.
decisions.description: Surface stock, margin and cash alerts so you know what to order, from whom and in what quantities.
decisions.alt: Freedoliapp decision dashboard with KPIs and decision cards.

trust.title: Designed for real operations, not experiments.

points.fba_workflows: Amazon FBA workflows you already know.
points.fba_workflows_desc: Purchase orders, shipments and inventory flows that match how Amazon sellers actually operate.
points.supplier_management_desc: Centralise supplier data, terms and communication instead of spreading it across chats and spreadsheets.
points.inventory_visibility_desc: See current and incoming stock across SKUs so you can avoid stockouts and overstock.
points.profit_tracking_desc: Combine costs, fees and sales so you know true profitability by product and order.

problem.title: Running an Amazon business shouldn’t require 10 different tools.
problem.description: Most teams run suppliers, orders and inventory across spreadsheets, chats and email. Data is scattered, and nobody fully trusts the numbers.

items.excel: Excel sheets for inventory and purchase orders.
items.shipping_docs: Shipping documents buried in email attachments.
items.inventory_spreadsheets: Separate spreadsheets just to track stock and profit.

solution.title: The Amazon operations control tower.
solution.description: Freedoliapp centralises suppliers, purchase orders, inventory and profit so your team can run operations from a single workspace.

points.visibility: Clear visibility across suppliers, stock and orders.
points.workflows: Structured workflows from quote to delivered units.
points.profit: Operational views that always connect back to profit.

modules.title: Everything your Amazon business needs in one place.
modules.description: A single workspace for sourcing, orders, inventory and profitability.

suppliers.title: Suppliers
suppliers.desc: Keep all supplier details, terms and notes in one structured view.

purchase_orders.title: Purchase Orders
purchase_orders.desc: Create, approve and track POs so production and shipments stay on schedule.

inventory.title: Inventory
inventory.desc: See current and incoming stock levels across SKUs so you can plan replenishments with confidence.

profit.title: Profit
profit.desc: Understand true profitability by product and order, not just top-line revenue.

decisions.title: Operational Decisions
decisions.desc: Get clear recommendations on what to order next, in what quantities and from which supplier.

assistant.title: Meet Freedoli, your operational assistant.
assistant.description: Use the assistant to explore the product, understand your numbers and find the next best action.

resources.title: Resources for Amazon operators
resources.description: Guides, templates and tools to help you run your Amazon operations with more control.

guides.title: Guides
guides.desc: Deep dives into supplier management, purchase orders, inventory and profitability.

templates.title: Templates
templates.desc: Practical spreadsheets and documents you can start with before moving into Freedoliapp.

tools.title: Tools
tools.desc: Calculators and utilities for Amazon operators, such as profit and inventory coverage.

final_cta.title: Ready to run your Amazon operations from one place?
final_cta.description: Start a free trial and see how Freedoliapp fits your current workflows.

analytics.title: Amazon analytics
analytics.desc: Import settlement data and see real revenue, fees and net by product.

pipeline.title: Product pipeline

finance.title: Financial clarity

dashboard.createProject: Create your first product to start the launch pipeline.
dashboard.openOrCreatePO: You have products; create a purchase order or open a project to move forward.
dashboard.reviewActivity: Review orders in progress or projects that need attention.

projects.openOrCreateOrder: Open a project to review phases or create an order for an already validated product.

orders.createOrLink: Create a purchase order or link it to a project to track production.

assistant.title: Assistant

answers.what_can_i_do: This screen lets you navigate and act by context: create or open projects, orders, review phases and viability. Use the sidebar buttons or each section's actions.
answers.next_step: The next step depends on your state: if you have no projects, create one; if you do, open orders or review activity. Dashboard and each screen show a recommendation.
answers.where_orders: Purchase orders (POs) are in the Orders section. Access from the sidebar or Dashboard.
answers.where_viability: Viability and margin are inside each project, in the viability/profitability tab. Open a project and look for «Viability» or «Margin».
answers.where_projects: Projects are in the Projects section. Access from the sidebar or Dashboard.
answers.what_means_state: PO statuses (draft, confirmed, shipped, etc.) and terms like FNSKU are in the help manual. Open «Help» in the top bar and search for «Orders» or «Amazon readiness».
answers.flow_project_po: The flow is: (1) Create a project in Projects, (2) Complete phases (viability, readiness), (3) Create a PO in Orders and link it to the project, (4) Track inventory and shipments. You can go to Projects or Orders from here.
answers.po_blocked: If a PO is blocked, check: order status, product Amazon readiness, pending tasks, and shipments. Open Orders and the PO detail to see alerts and tasks.
answers.what_missing_phase: To advance a phase you need to complete the current phase gates: product data, viability, Amazon readiness, tasks. The project view shows what's pending.

assistant.noMatch: No exact answer found. Try «What can I do here?» or «What's the next step?», or open the help manual.

amazonImport.description: We request and process the last 30 days of settlement activity. It can take a few minutes.
amazonImport.descriptionError: Something went wrong. You can retry the import or continue to the snapshot using any data already in your ledger.

amazonSnapshot.title: Understanding the numbers
amazonSnapshot.description: Figures come from posted ledger rows in the last 30 days. No activity yet is OK—you can still finish and explore the dashboard.

setupDone.title: What comes next
setupDone.description: Continue to the final step to save activation and open the dashboard. Then create your first product or project when you're ready.

complete.title: Ready to start

profitability.title: Profitability
profitability.long: The profitability calculator helps you determine if a product is viable to sell on Amazon. Enter expected costs and revenues to get the margin and decision (GO, RISKY, NO-GO).

selling_price.title: Selling Price
selling_price.short: Price at which you will sell the product on Amazon
selling_price.long: The selling price is the final price that the customer will pay for the product on Amazon. This price must include all costs and your desired profit.

cogs.title: COGS (Cost of Goods Sold)
cogs.long: COGS includes the unit cost of the product at the factory, excluding shipping and other costs. It's the price you pay the supplier per unit.

shipping_per_unit.title: Shipping per unit
shipping_per_unit.short: Shipping cost from factory to Amazon
shipping_per_unit.long: Shipping cost per unit from the factory to Amazon's warehouse (FBA). Includes international transport, customs, and handling.

referral_fee_percent.title: Referral Fee
referral_fee_percent.long: Amazon charges a referral fee on each sale. This percentage varies by product category (usually between 8% and 15%).

fba_fee_per_unit.title: FBA Fee per unit
fba_fee_per_unit.long: Amazon charges an FBA (Fulfillment by Amazon) fee per unit that includes storage, picking, packing, and shipping to the end customer.

ppc_per_unit.title: PPC per unit
ppc_per_unit.long: Average advertising cost (Pay-Per-Click) per unit sold. Calculate total advertising cost divided by number of units sold.

other_costs_per_unit.title: Other costs per unit
other_costs_per_unit.long: Any other variable cost per unit, such as custom packaging, labels, quality inspections, etc.

fixed_costs.title: Fixed Costs
fixed_costs.long: Fixed costs that are only paid once, such as product photography, listing creation, brand registration, etc. They are divided among all units sold.

margin.title: Margin
margin.long: Margin is the profit percentage you get over the selling price after subtracting all costs. A margin above 25% is considered good.

roi.title: ROI (Return on Investment)
roi.long: ROI indicates how much profit per euro invested. An ROI above 2.0 means you earn more than double what you invest.
roi.example: Ex: ROI of 2.5 means €2.50 profit for every €1 invested

amazon_ready.title: Amazon Ready
amazon_ready.short: Configuration to send products to Amazon
amazon_ready.long: The Amazon Ready section contains all the information needed to send products to Amazon FBA warehouses. Make sure all fields are completed before generating the Manufacturer Pack.

fnsku.title: FNSKU
fnsku.short: Amazon's unique code for the product
fnsku.long: FNSKU (Fulfillment Network Stock Keeping Unit) is a unique code that Amazon assigns to your product. It's needed to generate the labels that must be stuck on each unit.

gtin.title: GTIN
gtin.long: GTIN (Global Trade Item Number) is a unique barcode that identifies the product globally. It can be an EAN-13 (Europe) or UPC (United States).

units_per_carton.title: Units per carton
units_per_carton.short: Number of units that fit in a carton
units_per_carton.long: Number of individual units packed in each carton. This information is needed to generate carton labels and calculate space needed on the pallet.

cartons_per_pallet.title: Cartons per pallet
cartons_per_pallet.short: Number of cartons that fit on a pallet
cartons_per_pallet.long: Number of cartons that can be stacked on a standard pallet. This helps calculate space needed in the warehouse and optimize shipments.

purchase_orders.title: Purchase Orders
purchase_orders.long: Purchase orders (PO) are documents that formalize the purchase of products from suppliers. Here you can manage the entire lifecycle of orders, from creation to receipt.

po_number.title: PO Number
po_number.long: The PO number is a unique identifier automatically generated for each purchase order. It's used to reference the order in communications with suppliers and for tracking.

status.title: Order Status
status.long: Order status indicates which phase it's in: confirmed (confirmed), waiting_manufacturer (waiting for manufacturing), in_transit (in transit), received (received), etc.

tracking_number.title: Tracking Number
tracking_number.long: The tracking number allows you to track the shipment from the factory to the warehouse. Enter the code provided by the shipping company.

eta_date.title: Estimated Arrival Date
eta_date.long: The estimated arrival date (ETA) is the expected date when the shipment will arrive at Amazon's warehouse or your warehouse. Helps plan receipt and product launch.

research.title: Research Phase
research.long: The research phase is the first stage of the project, where you evaluate product viability. Here you capture product information, analyze competition, and make the initial decision (GO, RISKY, DISCARDED).

asin.title: Amazon ASIN
asin.long: ASIN (Amazon Standard Identification Number) is a unique 10-character identifier that Amazon assigns to each product. You can get it from the product URL on Amazon or enter it directly.

supplier_quotes.title: Supplier Quotes
supplier_quotes.short: Comparison of prices from different suppliers
supplier_quotes.long: Supplier quotes allow you to compare offers from different suppliers for the same product. You can add multiple quotes and compare prices, MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity), and conditions.

decision.title: Decision
decision.long: The decision indicates if the product is viable: GO (viable, continue), RISKY (viable but with risk), DISCARDED (not viable, discard). This decision is based on profitability and competition analysis.

finances.title: Finances
finances.long: The finances section allows you to record and manage all expenses and income related to your projects. You can create custom categories, view balance, and generate reports.

expenses.title: Expenses
expenses.long: Expenses include all costs related to projects: purchase orders, samples, advertising, tools, etc. You can assign them to a specific project or leave them global.

incomes.title: Income
incomes.long: Income is all the money you receive from product sales on Amazon. You can assign it to a specific project for detailed profitability tracking.

categories.title: Categories
categories.long: Categories allow you to organize your expenses and income. You can create custom categories like 'Advertising', 'Samples', 'Tools', etc., for better financial control.

recurring_expenses.title: Recurring Expenses
recurring_expenses.long: Recurring expenses are expenses that are automatically generated each month or period (such as subscriptions, rents, etc.). The system generates them automatically according to the configuration.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.short: Overview of all projects and activities
dashboard.long: The dashboard is the main page where you see an overview of all your projects, orders, tasks, and activities. You can customize the widgets that are displayed to adapt it to your needs.

widgets.title: Widgets
widgets.long: Widgets are elements displayed on the dashboard to provide quick information. You can enable or disable widgets and reorganize them according to your preferences.

sticky_notes.title: Sticky Notes
sticky_notes.long: Sticky notes allow you to create quick reminders. They can be global or associated with a specific project. You can convert them to tasks when necessary.

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.long: Tasks help you track pending actions. They can be associated with a specific project or be global. You can assign priority and due date.

inbox.title: Task Inbox
inbox.noWorkspace: Select a workspace to view tasks.

empty.title: No orders yet
empty.subtitle: Create your first order to start tracking production and inbound inventory.

orders.intro: Continue your project operations here: create and track POs linked to your projects, with logistics and documents.

toasts.noProject: This order has no linked project.
toasts.missingFnsku: The project has no FNSKU. Add it in the project Identifiers section.

detail.manufacturerPackHelp: Generate all documents needed to send to the manufacturer (PO, labels, packing list, carton labels).

messages.discovery: In Discovery, define the competitor ASIN and complete the snapshot to validate viability.
messages.noGtin: GTIN or GTIN exemption is missing. Assign an EAN/UPC or mark the product as exempt per Amazon approval.
messages.noAmazonSku: Amazon SKU is missing. Assign an Amazon SKU in the identifiers section.
messages.partialNoPoExempt: The product has GTIN exemption and Amazon SKU but still needs a purchase order (PO).
messages.partialNoPo: The product already has GTIN and Amazon SKU but still needs a purchase order (PO).
messages.ready: This product meets the basic requirements for Amazon.
messages.fallback: Amazon requirements are incomplete. Review GTIN, Amazon SKU and orders.

settings.title: Settings
settings.nameAndImageRequired: Name and signature image are required

gtinPool.description: Manage available EAN/UPC codes to assign to projects
gtinPool.confirmRelease: Are you sure you want to release this GTIN? It can be assigned to another project.

projects.codesAutoGenerated: Codes are generated automatically. The SKU will be used to identify the product on Amazon and in orders.

empty.title: No projects yet

economics.incompleteCallout: Data incomplete: add receipts / fix mapping / recompute.
economics.notEnoughData: Not enough data to show chart

idea.title: Idea & research
items: Clarify the product idea and target customer.
Collect 3–5 reference products on Amazon.
List key differentiators you want to test.

viability.title: Viability
items: Estimate landed cost per unit.
Check expected Amazon fees and margins.
Validate minimum order quantity vs. budget.

quotes.title: Supplier quotes
items: Request quotes from at least 3 suppliers.
Compare lead times, MOQs and payment terms.
Shortlist the 1–2 best options.

samples.title: Samples
items: Order samples from shortlisted suppliers.
Review quality, packaging and defects.
Decide which supplier you want to go with.

po.title: Purchase order
items: Confirm final specs and packaging.
Negotiate final price and payment terms.
Send and confirm the purchase order (PO).

shipment.title: Shipment
items: Choose Incoterms and shipping method.
Plan labels and carton packing.
Book freight and share dates with supplier.

amazon.title: Send to Amazon / go live
items: Create or update the Amazon listing.
Create the inbound shipment in Seller Central.
Monitor check-in and first sales.
